response.justification: Candidate is an experienced senior full-stack developer with strong web, mobile, and DLang backend experience, including low-latency micro-services at Sociomantic and a geospatial/GIS platform (GISCollective). However, the role requires modern C++ or Rust as a primary skill, and the resume shows only limited C/C++ exposure from a 6-month stint at National Instruments back in 2014-2015, with no Rust experience. The geospatial background and low-latency systems work are relevant, but the core language requirement is a significant gap that would likely require substantial ramp-up time.
strengths: Geospatial/GIS domain experience via GISCollective and Open Green Map platform
Low-latency micro-services experience handling millions of requests/second at Sociomantic
Strong engineering fundamentals: TDD, CI/CD, distributed systems, Linux
concerns: No recent modern C++ experience; only brief C/C++ work at NI in 2014-2015
Career trajectory is web/full-stack focused (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang), not systems/pipeline engineering in C++
